context: W1 correction. GLM loaded with num_gpu=30 (partial offload). Testing whether template leak is stop-token fixable or structural.

probes.prompt: Classify this input: Database connection fails after 30 seconds. Output only a single word: bug, feature, or unknown.
probes.thinking_preview: Got it, let's tackle this classification task. The user wants to classify the input...

probes.description: GLM with both begin_of_box and end_of_box as stop tokens
probes.note: Empty output — begin_of_box stop token fired immediately, cutting off all visible content. GLM wraps output in <|begin_of_box|>...<|end_of_box|>, so the begin token fires before content starts.
probes.thinking_preview: Got it, let's see. The user wants to classify...

probes.prompt: What is your model name? Reply in one sentence.
probes.raw_content: I am the GLM large language model developed by Zhipu AI.
probes.note: No template leak on identity probe (no structured output wrapping). Clean English response. Correct self-identification.
probes.thinking_preview_unicode: 首先看问题，要求是给出模型名...
probes.thinking_note: GLM's thinking field contains Chinese reasoning even when prompt and response are in English. This consumes context budget proportionally.

probes.description: GLM with only end_of_box as stop token (the W2 fix path)
probes.note: Output includes begin_of_box prefix but stops before end_of_box. Content after stripping prefix: 'feature'. The W2 dispatcher can strip this prefix.
probes.w2_fix: Use <|end_of_box|> as stop token + post-process strip of <|begin_of_box|> prefix from response content.
probes.classification_unstable: Response was 'feature' instead of 'bug' (temperature-dependent). Functional for classification tasks but output varies.

w2_fix_path.dispatcher_change: Add GLM family to model-family stop token lookup table. Add response post-processor for GLM.
w2_fix_path.feasibility: Confirmed feasible. Probe 4 demonstrates the fix path works — clean content after prefix strip.

thinking_field_findings.language_behavior: Chinese for identity/simple prompts. English for classification tasks. Language selection appears task-dependent.
thinking_field_findings.w4_cascade_impact: If GLM serves as Auditor, thinking field reasoning is in Chinese — not visible in cascade artifacts unless explicitly captured. Content field is in English.

evidence_summary: GLM template leak is stop-token fixable. W2 dispatcher adds <|end_of_box|> to GLM stop tokens and strips <|begin_of_box|> from response. GLM's thinking field is present in /api/chat responses (new vs W0 — either Ollama 0.20.2 added it or it was missed). Thinking language is Chinese for identity probes, English for classification. Template leak is NOT structural — it's a known GLM output pattern that Ollama doesn't handle by default.
